items.text:
@maya_stargazer

Okay, I have to tell you all about last night! I went to the Stargazers Society's Autumn Sky Party up on the roof of Hensley Science Hall, and it was honestly the best thing I've done all semester!!

For $6 you get in, borrow one of their big telescopes for the whole night, and they hand you a hot cider on the way up — worth every cent. I got there right at 8:00 PM when the doors opened, and by the time Saturn swung into view around 9:30 the whole roof was packed. The club members actually walk you through finding things yourself instead of just pointing, which is why I finally understood how to read a star chart. Pro tip: bring a jacket, it gets freezing up there once the sun's gone!

The next one is November 14, and they cap it at 40 people so it fills fast. Grab your ticket from the Physics Department office before it sells out — you seriously do not want to miss Saturn's rings. Who's coming with me?? #campuslife #stargazing

questions.stem: According to the post, what does the $6 fee include?

options.A: A star chart and a borrowed warm jacket
options.B: A guided tour of the Physics Department office
options.C: Admission, telescope use, and a hot cider
options.D: A reserved seat and a printed sky map

questions.explanation: The post says that for $6 you 'get in, borrow one of their big telescopes for the whole night, and they hand you a hot cider' — admission, telescope use, and cider. A jacket is something the writer advises bringing yourself, and the tour, reserved seat, and sky map are never mentioned.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about how the Stargazers Society runs its events?

options.A: They rely on professional astronomers to guide viewing
options.B: They help attendees locate objects on their own
options.C: They discourage beginners from handling the telescopes
options.D: They focus on lectures rather than hands-on viewing

questions.explanation: The writer notes the club members 'walk you through finding things yourself instead of just pointing,' which is why they finally understood how to read a star chart — implying the society teaches attendees to locate objects independently. Options C and D reverse this hands-on approach, and A introduces professional astronomers never mentioned.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about getting a ticket for the November 14 event?

options.A: Students can buy tickets at the rooftop entrance
options.B: Students will receive a free hot cider voucher
options.C: Students should reserve a ticket before spots fill
options.D: Students must be Physics majors in order to attend

questions.explanation: The post says the event is capped at 40 people 'so it fills fast' and urges readers to grab a ticket 'before it sells out,' implying students should act early because spots are limited. Tickets come from the Physics Department office (not the rooftop), no voucher is mentioned, and attendance is open to everyone, not just Physics majors.

items.text:
Notice: New Package Pickup Procedure

Effective Monday, October 6, all student packages will be processed through the new Package Center on the lower level of Whitman Hall, replacing the front-desk system previously used in each residence hall.

Packages will be held for pickup for up to 10 days. After that period, uncollected items will be returned to sender, and a $3 restocking fee will be charged to the student's account. To retrieve a package, students must present a valid student ID together with the tracking number sent by email.

Pickup hours are as follows:
- Monday to Friday: 8:00 AM to 7:00 PM
- Saturday: 10:00 AM to 2:00 PM
- Sunday: closed

Please note that perishable items cannot be stored and should be collected on the day they arrive. Oversized deliveries requiring special handling may incur an additional $5 fee.

Questions may be directed to the Package Center at packages@whitman.edu or extension 4420.

format_metadata.title: New Package Pickup Procedure
format_metadata.location: Package Center, lower level of Whitman Hall

questions.stem: According to the notice, what happens to a package that is not collected within 10 days?

options.A: It is transferred to another residence hall desk
options.B: It is stored for an extra ten business days
options.C: It is delivered straight to the student's room
options.D: It is sent back and a fee applied

questions.explanation: The notice states that after the 10-day period 'uncollected items will be returned to sender, and a $3 restocking fee will be charged.' The residence-hall desk system was replaced, no extra holding period is offered, and packages are never delivered to rooms.

questions.stem: In the notice, the word 'retrieve' is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation: 'To retrieve a package, students must present a valid student ID' means to pick up or collect the package, so 'collect' is the closest match. 'Return' is the opposite action, and 'inspect' and 'purchase' change the meaning entirely.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about perishable items sent to the Package Center?

options.A: They are exempt from paying the restocking fee
options.B: They cannot be held past their arrival day
options.C: They require an extra $5 handling fee
options.D: They are stored in a special refrigerated locker

questions.explanation: The notice says perishable items 'cannot be stored and should be collected on the day they arrive,' implying they will not be kept beyond their arrival day like ordinary packages. No fee exemption is stated, the $5 fee applies to oversized deliveries, and no refrigerated storage is offered.
